pulls.title: more updates (hybrid disambiguation, KE sections descriptions, ML-KEM-512 omission rationale)
pulls.author: panva
pulls.body:
- Clarified "hybrid" terminology disambiguation in the Introduction
- Added descriptive text to Key Encryption algorithm sections
- Expanded Security Considerations with ML-KEM-512 omission rationale

pulls.title: post-alignment updates
pulls.author: panva
pulls.body:
- **Removed ChaCha20Poly1305 AEAD ciphersuites.** Dropped `HPKE-9`, `HPKE-9-KE`, `HPKE-11`, `HPKE-11-KE`, `HPKE-13`, `HPKE-13-KE`, `HPKE-14`, `HPKE-14-KE`, `HPKE-16`, `HPKE-16-KE`, and the ChaCha20Poly1305 variants of `HPKE-15(-KE)` / `HPKE-17(-KE)`. Remaining registrations all use AES-256-GCM.
- **Intentional gaps in Algorithm Name numbering** so a given identifier (e.g. `HPKE-10`) always denotes the same HPKE KEM/KDF/AEAD combination across JOSE and COSE. Added a note in the IANA Considerations explaining the gaps.
- **New "Security Strength" subsection** in Security Considerations covering NIST PQ security levels for ML-KEM-768/1024, classical floor provided by P-256/X25519/P-384 in the PQ/T hybrids, and rationale for SHAKE256 + AES-256-GCM (with reference to CNSA 2.0).
- **Additional security considerations** motivating PQ/T hybrid confidentiality (HNDL) and requiring that in a General JWE JSON Serialization with multiple recipients, all recipients MUST use a quantum-resistant Key Management algorithm.
- **Refactored JSON Web Key Representation section.** Points "AKP" key type to Section 3 of `I-D.ietf-cose-dilithium`; clarifies that `alg` is required and identifies both the HPKE ciphersuite and Integrated vs. Key Encryption usage; specifies `pub`/`priv` as base64url-encoded (no padding, per RFC 4648 Section 5) outputs of `SerializePublicKey()`/`SerializePrivateKey()` from Section 4 of `I-D.ietf-hpke-hpke`.
- **New informative references:** `I-D.ietf-pquip-pqc-engineers` and `CNSA2.0`.
- **Authors:** added Hannes Tschofenig (University of the Bundeswehr Munich) and Tirumaleswar Reddy (Nokia); updated Hannes' organization.
